$comment: C 6.2.3 tag/ordinary namespace independence (companion to tag_namespace). A struct TAG `S` and an ordinary OBJECT `S` share a spelling in one scope chain WITHOUT colliding: `struct S a;` reads the tag (type-position tag-ref arm), bare `S` reads the ordinary int local. They resolve to different entities (struct type vs int object) — a.v (40) + S (2) = 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: a single-namespace table collides the local `int S` with the tag `S` (S_RedeclaredSymbol) and the compile fails loud, so the example never links.
